steps.title: Identify what the answer must do
steps.content: The researchers concluded that F13 (which had a higher oxygen-18 concentration than M03) formed at a significantly lower temperature. The logic requires that higher oxygen-18 concentration = lower formation temperature. A supporting finding must establish this inverse relationship between oxygen-18 and temperature.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — As the concentration of oxygen-18 in water decreases, water temperature decreases and nautilid shell growth slows.: This links decreasing oxygen-18 to decreasing temperature — the same direction for both variables. That would mean higher oxygen-18 = higher temperature, which directly contradicts the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice B — As water depth increases, both the concentration of oxygen-18 and the water temperature decrease.: Both oxygen-18 and temperature decrease together with depth, meaning they move in the same direction. Higher oxygen-18 would then correspond to higher temperature — the opposite of what the conclusion requires. ✗
  • Choice C — As water depth increases, the concentration of oxygen-18 increases and water temperature decreases.: As depth increases, oxygen-18 goes up while temperature goes down — an inverse relationship. This means higher oxygen-18 = lower temperature, exactly supporting the conclusion that F13's higher oxygen-18 means it formed in colder water. ✓
  • Choice D — As water temperature increases, nautilids are able to concentrate more oxygen-18 into their shells.: Higher temperature leads to more oxygen-18, so higher oxygen-18 = higher temperature. This contradicts the conclusion. ✗
C is correct.
